mechanism_narrative: Declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through reduced aggregate demand and profit margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:July 2009 labor market still contracting from 2008 crisis
failure_condition: Monthly PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.04 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at near-zero levels (FEDFUNDS < 0.5%) signals an extreme monetary accommodation regime where equity risk premiums compress and capital flows into equities as alternative to cash. This produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as portfolio rebalancing accelerates.
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy regime
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 40% of periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or Sharpe ratio of the signal is negative over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) combined with expanding housing starts (HOUST) indicate strengthening consumer demand and construction activity. This joint signal of demand recovery drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days through improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 500k units annualized
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 532k units in May 2009, RSAFS at $342.1B in June 2009
failure_condition: Joint signal triggers do not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 58% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates as manufacturing capacity utilization recovers from crisis lows. Accelerating INDPRO signals improving supply-side efficiency and corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as market reprices earnings expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.40 in June 2009, recovering from 2008 lows
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO acceleration does not produce positive 5-day SPX returns in at least 52% of triggered instances, or correlation between INDPRO momentum and 5-day returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 9.5% signals persistent labor market slack that constrains wage growth and consumer confidence. This elevated unemployment regime produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through reduced earnings growth expectations and lower equity risk appetite.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 45% of months when UNRATE > 9.5%, or the negative return differential versus low-unemployment periods is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL remains subdued (year-over-year growth < 2.5%) while FEDFUNDS is near zero, indicating the Fed maintains accommodative policy with no imminent tightening. This policy certainty produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through stable discount rate expectations and reduced policy uncertainty.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 214.46 in June 2009, FEDFUNDS at 0.21%
failure_condition: Joint signal does not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 56% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between inflation and rates is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth accelerates from crisis trough, signaling economic recovery that improves corporate earnings visibility. Positive GDP growth momentum produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through multiple expansion and reduced recession risk premium.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at $11,360.5B in Q1 2009, recovering from 2008 contraction
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters do not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 60% of instances, or the lead-lag relationship shows GDP growth lagging SPX returns by more than 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling stagflationary pressure or demand destruction. This joint deterioration produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through earnings compression and multiple contraction.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month change < -0.5% AND UNRATE month-over-month change > 0.2pp
evidence_refs: fact:Both series available monthly for real-time monitoring
failure_condition: Joint signal triggers do not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 54% of instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression, indicating the signals operate independently rather than jointly.
